posts.text:
Reuters News - RESCUERS FROM OTHER COUNTRIES ARRIVING TO VENEZUELA IN THE COMING HOURS, INTERIM PRESIDENT DELCY RODRIGUEZ SAYS
- VENEZUELA PRESIDENT RODRIGUEZ THANKS TRUMP FOR SUPPORT AFTER EARTHQUAKE
posts.summary: Venezuela's interim president has announced incoming international rescue assistance following an earthquake and expressed gratitude to Trump for support, though the post provides no specific details about potential impacts on Venezuela's oil production or exports. Any oil market implications would depend on the earthquake's severity and damage to Venezuela's critical oil infrastructure, which is not described in this announcement.

posts.text:
Iran has exported 40 million barrels of crude oil since 2026-06-15. Exactly half of that amount shipped out in a single day; which was last Friday (2026-06-19).

#OOTT #IranWar #Tankers
posts.summary: Iran exported 40 million barrels of crude oil between June 15-19, 2026, with half of that volume shipped on a single day (June 19), suggesting a significant spike in export activity. The concentrated export surge could indicate a strategic effort to move large quantities of oil quickly, potentially reflecting geopolitical or market pressures affecting Iran's crude sales.

posts.text:
The U.S., Qatar, Nigeria, and Algeria sent a letter to the European Union:

@SecretaryWright: “Reform your regulations or you’re going to lead to massively higher energy prices in Europe…let’s bring common sense into energy.” https://t.co/HPDOf66Gl7
posts.summary: Four major energy-producing nations (the U.S., Qatar, Nigeria, and Algeria) have urged the EU to reform its energy regulations to prevent higher prices in Europe. The letter, referenced by Secretary Wright, advocates for what these countries characterize as a more pragmatic approach to energy policy.

posts.text:
Hormuz transits are ~2x pre-MOU levels (more in total oil volume), but:

1) still nowhere near sustainable prewar levels
2) many of those transits are still dark

So why aren't they higher yet (we know many more would like to be exiting), and why still so many dark?
posts.summary: Despite increased Strait of Hormuz oil transits since the MOU agreement, volumes remain significantly below pre-war levels and many shipments operate with disabled tracking, suggesting continued constraints on global oil supply and market uncertainty. The gap between current and potential transit levels indicates that risk factors or logistical barriers are still preventing a full return to normal crude exports.

posts.text:
Brent crude is now a pre-war levels at around $72.5 a barrel. 

The Brent futures market peaked on April 30th at $126.41 a barrel.
posts.summary: Brent crude has declined to pre-war levels around $72.50 per barrel, representing a significant drop from its peak of $126.41 on April 30th. The roughly 43% price decrease reflects a substantial shift in oil market conditions from earlier in the year.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic cautiously improves

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz traffic stayed active on 23 June, with 31 verified crossings across commercial and energy-linked vessels. West-to-east movements dominated, while Iranian, Omani and IMO routes all remained in use. The Strait appears https://t.co/TOe354A89q
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz traffic showed continued activity with 31 verified vessel crossings on June 23, suggesting stable passage for energy supplies through this critical chokepoint. The maintenance of multiple shipping routes indicates no significant disruption to oil flows, which supports market stability in a geopolitically sensitive region.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic sees a sharp d/d uptick

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz crossings rose to 70 on 24 June, up 105% day on day, as demining efforts advanced and operators increasingly used the Omani route. Commercial traffic accounted for most activity, with 53 transits, while low-risk https://t.co/Afhj0gqoHt
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz crossings surged 105% to 70 transits on June 24, driven by demining progress and increased use of the Omani route, suggesting improved shipping activity through this critical chokepoint for global oil markets. The majority of traffic was commercial transit, indicating a resumption of normal trade flows through the region.

posts.text: The sale nets Iran not a few million as JD Vance suggested, but ~$3 billion https://t.co/PV9ft5FjoJ
posts.summary: Patrick De Haan corrects a claim by JD Vance, stating that Iran's oil sale generates approximately $3 billion rather than just a few million dollars. The correction highlights a significant discrepancy in understanding the financial scale of Iran's oil transactions.

posts.text:
Saudi Arabia appears to be about re-starting oil loadings inside the Persian Gulf. 

Three Saudi-owned in ballast VLCCs are concentrating around the Ju’aymah terminal (it has 6 single point moorings).

If the loadings go ahead, it would be the first known since early March.
posts.summary: Saudi Arabia may resume oil loadings at its Ju'aymah terminal in the Persian Gulf for the first time since early March, with three large tankers currently positioned at the facility. This resumption could signal a shift in Saudi oil export activity after a months-long pause.

posts.text: BREAKING: GasBuddy Projects Second Most Expensive July 4 at the Pump Despite Six Weeks of Declines… National average price of gasoline on July 4 projected at $3.75/gal; on track for second most expensive Independence Day at the pump ever
posts.summary: GasBuddy projects the national average gasoline price on July 4 will be $3.75 per gallon, marking the second most expensive Independence Day at the pump on record despite six weeks of price declines. The projection suggests sustained upward pressure on fuel prices heading into the holiday period.

posts.text: Did we reach peak Fed hawkishness on Monday?  Since then, the US 2-year Treasury yield has fallen from a 16-month high of 4.23% to around 4.10%. While still trading above the current Fed funds rate, the move suggests investors are beginning to question whether additional https://t.co/RHtENPEvzl
posts.summary: Following the Fed's recent hawkish stance, the decline in 2-year Treasury yields from 4.23% to 4.10% suggests investors may be reassessing expectations for further rate increases, which could have implications for oil demand and pricing. Lower Treasury yields typically correlate with weaker economic growth prospects and reduced energy consumption.

https://t.co/a6424MFlip https://t.co/QXo0BN2LrT
posts.summary: WTI crude oil has declined below the $70 per barrel mark, signaling a downward price movement in the oil market.

posts.text: EIA: Miserable report for refiners with 3.1 m build in distillate stocks and 2.1-m build in gasoline. Gas demand measurement of 8.775-mbd will restart demand destruction dialogue. But refiners, blenders and importers are no longer bringing 10-mbd of gas to market, so be careful.
posts.summary: The EIA reported significant builds in distillate and gasoline stocks while gasoline demand fell to 8.775 million barrels per day, signaling potential demand destruction concerns. However, refiners are currently supplying less gasoline to the market than historical levels, which could limit downside price pressure despite the weak demand data.

posts.text: U.S. PETROLEUM inventories depleted by another 10 million barrels last week and have fallen by a total of 151 million barrels since the United States and Israel attacked Iran at the end of February. Total stocks of crude and refined products (including in the Strategic Petroleum https://t.co/DAzGbYxYd3
posts.summary: U.S. petroleum inventories declined by 10 million barrels last week, continuing a downward trend of 151 million barrels total since late February. The significant depletion of crude and refined product stocks could put upward pressure on oil prices if supply continues to tighten.

posts.text: Oil prices are retreating as U.S.-Iran talks reduce immediate supply fears, but uncertainty remains around Hormuz and a final nuclear agreement. Meanwhile, China’s crude imports plunged 29% year-over-year in May, adding pressure to the market outlook. #Oil #Iran #China
posts.summary: Oil prices are declining due to reduced supply concerns from U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks, though risks remain around the Strait of Hormuz and nuclear negotiations. The weakness is compounded by a sharp 29% year-over-year drop in Chinese crude imports in May, which is dampening demand outlook.

posts.text: Share prices for two of America's largest gasoline retailers, $MUSA and $CASY, are down 5-6 percent this morning after President Trump threatened to use the Department of Justice to investigate gasoline prices.
posts.summary: President Trump's threat to investigate gasoline prices through the Department of Justice has triggered a market sell-off in major U.S. gasoline retailers, with shares of Murphy USA and Casey's General Stores declining 5-6 percent. The development signals potential regulatory pressure on the retail gasoline sector.

posts.text:
Oil Prices Retreat to Pre-War Level as Supply Concerns Ease

Crude oil prices and calendar spreads have largely reverted to pre-war levels as traders anticipate a rapid normalisation of exports through the Strait of Hormuz and the smooth replenishment of depleted oil inventories https://t.co/kQu7Q9hU3r
posts.summary: Crude oil prices have declined to pre-war levels as market participants expect the Strait of Hormuz exports to normalize and oil inventories to be replenished, easing prior supply concerns.

posts.text:
Global primary energy demand is set to rise by 23% to 2050.

Global primary energy demand is set to increase from 312 million barrels of oil equivalent per day (mboe/d) in 2025 to nearly 383 mboe/d in 2050. This is an increase of around 23% over the outlook period, or 0.8% p.a. https://t.co/zPZs7aDTYE
posts.summary: Global energy demand is projected to grow 23% by 2050, reaching 383 million barrels of oil equivalent per day from 312 mboe/d in 2025, signaling sustained long-term growth in overall energy consumption that will likely support continued oil demand.

posts.text: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June. #TodayInEnergy https://t.co/9YwNkZ90T3 https://t.co/00Ku1HWU6y
posts.summary: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories declined during June, indicating reduced stockpiles in the market. This decrease could suggest increased demand or constrained supply in the domestic oil market.

posts.text:
Heatwaves can put major strain on energy systems as demand for cooling sends power use soaring.
 
In this 2025 podcast, IEA’s Brian Motherway looks at the rapid growth in air conditioning demand globally – and how to keep cool without overloading grids 👉 https://t.co/mDJZ2DMM9d https://t.co/3FrQEUeGhk
posts.summary: Rising global air conditioning demand during heatwaves strains electrical grids and increases power consumption, which has implications for energy system capacity and grid reliability management. The IEA highlights the need to balance cooling demand with grid stability to prevent energy system overload.

posts.summary: Oil prices have declined to pre-war levels as crude flows from the Gulf region increase. The rising supply from Gulf producers is contributing to downward pressure on global oil prices.

posts.text:
East Asian refiners seek sanctions clarity before buying Iranian crude

🔷 South Korea, Japan, Thai, Taiwan refiners seek sanctions clarity
🔷 Refinery configuration adjustments needed for Iranian crude
🔷 Platts assessed South Pars at $5.11/b discount to DFC
posts.summary: East Asian refiners are hesitant to purchase Iranian crude until sanctions regulations are clarified, which could limit potential buyers for Iranian oil despite current pricing discounts of around $5.11 per barrel. The refiners' need to make operational adjustments for Iranian crude processing adds another barrier to increased import volumes from Iran.

posts.text:
Europe has a narrow window to rebuild gas storage before winter — and it is running behind 

EU storage stood at 46% of capacity as of 23 June, roughly 15 bcm below the five-year average. At the rate injections have been running, the end-October fill would reach approximately 75% https://t.co/HhsjbmKpqM
posts.summary: EU natural gas storage is significantly below historical levels and is on track to reach only 75% capacity by end-October, potentially leaving Europe vulnerable to supply shortages during winter. This storage deficit has implications for European energy security and could affect broader energy markets, though direct oil market impacts are not addressed in the provided text.
